title: Rectal bleeding
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Defines acute adult hospital lower gastrointestinal bleeding as bright or dark red blood per rectum, clots per rectum, or blood mixed with stool, while warning that brisk bleeding should not automatically be ascribed to haemorrhoids.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped acute lower-GI-bleeding presentation terms and blood colour or relation-to-stool fields without attributing a cause.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines acute adult hospital lower gastrointestinal bleeding as bright or dark red blood per rectum, clots per rectum, or blood mixed with stool, while warning that brisk bleeding should not automatically be ascribed to haemorrhoids.

source.title: Diagnosis and management of acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: guidelines from the British Society of Gastroenterology

exact_section.heading: Scope of the guideline and background presentation description

red_flags.text: Supports routine observations, full history and examination including digital rectal examination, appropriate blood tests, heart rate, systolic blood pressure, haemoglobin, and previous lower-GI-bleeding admission context.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-recorded examination, DRE, observations, blood pressure, heart rate, haemoglobin or CBC, and previous-bleed assessment fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports routine observations, full history and examination including digital rectal examination, appropriate blood tests, heart rate, systolic blood pressure, haemoglobin, and previous lower-GI-bleeding admission context.

source.title: Diagnosis and management of acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: guidelines from the British Society of Gastroenterology

exact_section.heading: Management algorithm — initial assessment and Oakland variables

red_flags.text: Supports documenting whether anticoagulant or antiplatelet treatment is relevant to acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding; medicine interruption, reversal, and restart instructions are deliberately not mapped.
red_flags.rationale: Supports documenting anticoagulant relevance only; no medicine change or reversal instruction is mapped.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting whether anticoagulant or antiplatelet treatment is relevant to acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding; medicine interruption, reversal, and restart instructions are deliberately not mapped.

source.title: Diagnosis and management of acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: guidelines from the British Society of Gastroenterology

exact_section.heading: Abstract and recommendations — anticoagulant and antiplatelet context

red_flags.text: Supports clinician assessment of haemodynamic stability, major versus minor bleeding context, and qualified clinician-decided hospital or outpatient follow-up documentation without calculating a score or inserting disposition advice.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped haemodynamic-stability prompts and clinician-decided follow-up or referral documentation in qualified acute contexts; no score or disposition is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of haemodynamic stability, major versus minor bleeding context, and qualified clinician-decided hospital or outpatient follow-up documentation without calculating a score or inserting disposition advice.

source.title: Diagnosis and management of acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: guidelines from the British Society of Gastroenterology

red_flags.text: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped rectal-bleeding combinations with age, weight-loss, and bowel-habit qualifiers as prompts only; no cancer diagnosis is asserted.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.

source_limitations: The BSG source excludes uncomplicated primary-care LGIB and is limited to acute adult hospital presentations.

Amount or frequency, melaena or black-stool summary, dizziness or syncope, pain on defecation, anal inspection, family history, prior colonoscopy, coagulation testing, generic previous-record review, fixed follow-up intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.

Diagnosis, bleeding source, severity, haemodynamic interpretation, risk-score calculation, investigation choice, treatment, transfusion, medicine changes, referral urgency, and disposition remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
